public.kg_nodes.name: Python 3.12 Release Summary
public.kg_nodes.summary:
{"brief_summary": "Python 3.12, released October 2, 2023, introduces significant language, interpreter, and standard library enhancements focused on usability, correctness, and performance. Key updates include more powerful f-strings, ergonomic generic type syntax, a per-interpreter GIL, comprehension inlining, and extensive performance improvements across various modules, alongside security and C API changes.", "detailed_summary": "## Language Changes
- More powerful f-string implementation (PEP 701) allows any valid Python expression, including quote reuse, multi-line expressions, comments, and backslashes, leading to more precise error messages.
- New, more ergonomic syntax for generic types and type aliases (PEP 695) introduces compact syntax for generic classes/functions (e.g., `class list[T]:`) and a `type` statement for aliases (`type Point = tuple[float, float]`), supporting lazy evaluation and an "annotation scope".
- A new `typing.override()` decorator (PEP 698) was added for static type checkers. ## Interpreter Improvements
- Per-interpreter GIL (PEP 684) allows Python programs to use multiple CPU cores via sub-interpreters with unique GILs (currently C-API only, Python API planned for 3.13).
- Low-impact monitoring API (`sys.monitoring`) (PEP 669) for tools like profilers.
- Comprehension inlining (PEP 709) for dict, list, and set comprehensions speeds them up by up to two times, though this alters tracebacks and `locals()` behavior.
- Improved error messages for `NameError`, `ImportError`, and `SyntaxError`, offering suggestions for correction. ## Standard Library Updates
- The `distutils` package has been removed (PEP 632).
- `setuptools` is no longer pre-installed in virtual environments created with `venv`.
- The `asynchat`, `asyncore`, and `imp` modules are removed.
- Performance improvements across several modules, including `asyncio` (up to 75% speedup), `isinstance()` checks against runtime-checkable protocols (2-20x faster), `re.sub()` and `re.subn()` (2-3x faster for group references), and `tokenize` (up to 64% faster).
- Experimental support for the BOLT binary optimizer can improve performance by 1-5%.
- Security enhanced by replacing several `hashlib` implementations (SHA1, MD5, etc.) with formally verified code from the HACL* project, using OpenSSL versions as primary and built-ins as fallbacks. ## Other Notable Changes
- PEP 688 makes the buffer protocol accessible from Python via the `__buffer__()` method and the new `collections.abc.Buffer` ABC.
- The `pathlib.Path` class now supports subclassing, and `os` has improved Windows support.
- `sqlite3` and `uuid` modules gain command-line interfaces.
- New functions include `itertools.batched()` and `math.sumprod()`.
- `sum()` now uses Neumaier summation for improved float accuracy.
- `slice` objects are now hashable.
- PEP 623 removes `wstr` from C-level Unicode objects, reducing every `str` object's size by at least 8 bytes.
- `tarfile` extraction methods now have a `filter` argument for security (PEP 706), which will default to `'data'` in Python 3.14.
- CPython now supports the Linux `perf` profiler. ## C API Changes
- Introduces an "Unstable" tier (PEP 697).
- Immortal objects to bypass reference counting (PEP 683).
- Removes the legacy Unicode APIs based on `Py_UNICODE*` (PEP 623).
- Many functions and modules are deprecated, with removal scheduled for Python 3.13 through 3.17, including many modules listed in PEP 594 (e.g., `aifc`, `cgi`, `crypt`)."}

view_definitions.public.kg_graph_view:
SELECT id AS user_id,
    jsonb_build_object('nodes', COALESCE(( SELECT jsonb_agg(jsonb_build_object('id', n.id, 'name', n.name, 'group', n.source_type, 'summary', n.summary, 'tags', n.tags, 'url', n.url, 'date', COALESCE(n.node_date::text, ''::text))) AS jsonb_agg
           FROM kg_nodes n
          WHERE n.user_id = u.id), '[]'::jsonb), 'links', COALESCE(( SELECT jsonb_agg(jsonb_build_object('source', l.source_node_id, 'target', l.target_node_id, 'relation', l.relation, 'weight', l.weight, 'link_type', l.link_type, 'description', l.description)) AS jsonb_agg
           FROM kg_links l
          WHERE l.user_id = u.id), '[]'::jsonb)) AS graph_data
   FROM kg_users u;
view_definitions.public.kg_user_stats:
SELECT id AS user_id,
    render_user_id,
    display_name,
    ( SELECT count(*) AS count
           FROM kg_nodes n
          WHERE n.user_id = u.id) AS node_count,
    ( SELECT count(*) AS count
           FROM kg_links l
          WHERE l.user_id = u.id) AS link_count,
    COALESCE(( SELECT array_agg(DISTINCT unnested.unnested) AS array_agg
           FROM kg_nodes n2,
            LATERAL unnest(n2.tags) unnested(unnested)
          WHERE n2.user_id = u.id), '{}'::text[]) AS all_tags
   FROM kg_users u;
view_definitions.public.rag_sandbox_stats:
SELECT id,
    user_id,
    name,
    description,
    icon,
    color,
    default_quality,
    last_used_at,
    created_at,
    updated_at,
    ( SELECT count(*) AS count
           FROM rag_sandbox_members m
          WHERE m.sandbox_id = s.id) AS member_count
   FROM rag_sandboxes s;
